GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Bambi Northwood-Blyth passes the time with Longines in lavender", "A collectible for lovers of form and function.", "Ever the purveyors of exceptional design, Swiss watchmaker Longines's latest watch launch is a study in subtle luxury; classic elegance imagined for the modern woman. Inspired by the breathtaking natural beauty and colour palette captured within our fine shores – rolling hills of lavender, jacaranda trees with laden branches or the dusty pastels visible at sunset along epic coastlines, the Record Collection – Australian Limited Edition is exclusively available here. A lavender field in north eastern Tasmania, Bridestowe, was the perfect location for model Bambi Northwood-Blyth to explore the notion that time is fleeting, and something to be revelled in, wearing the highly covetable accessory. "Time is the one true luxury. The thing you can't save for. Or gift. Or take." she muses, in the film for Vogue. Bambi has found global success and currently calls New York City home, but there's an ease to her beauty and charm that feels uniquely Australian. The Record collection boasts sophisticated style, with a bezel featuring 54 diamonds, a luminescent mother-of-pearl dial and brilliant-cut diamond hour markers, but timeless elegance isn't the only hallmark. There's also high performance. In a first for the brand, Longines Record collection has been awarded chronometer certification by the Official Swiss Chronometer Testing Institute – which recognises extreme accuracy and precision in timekeeping. "Nature, always the best teacher, shows us how fleeting time is. That it stops for nothing. And that's what makes it beautiful, elegant. There is such magic in that unrelenting truth. That it keeps ticking no matter what," says Northwood-Blyth. The individually numbered timepiece (there are only 35 in existence) comes with three interchangeable alligator straps, in black, white and violet, which you can switch out yourself according to an outfit, mood or whim."
